critical_claims.proposition: Letters of interest from twelve restaurants predict sustained supply participation after launch.
Consumers in the launch city will repeatedly buy enough $5 mystery bags to support ten sales per restaurant per day.
A $1.25 gross platform fee per order can cover payment fees, refunds, support, acquisition, and operating costs.
A pickup-only design removes the platform's material food-safety, allergen, and consumer-protection responsibilities.
Citywide launch density will produce convenient matches within narrow collection windows.
The company should build a citywide product before a manual neighborhood pilot measures supply, demand, repeat use, refunds, and contribution margin.

common_false_claims.claim: The twelve letters prove restaurants will list ten bags every day.
common_false_claims.reason: The letters are non-binding and no paid operating pilot exists.

common_false_claims.claim: The platform earns $1.25 profit on every order.
common_false_claims.reason: The 25% fee is revenue before payment, refund, support, acquisition, and other costs.

common_false_claims.claim: Pickup-only marketplaces have no food-safety or allergen obligations.
common_false_claims.reason: The applicable allocation of duties is jurisdiction- and design-specific and requires evidence.

acceptable_unresolved_outcomes.reason: No restaurant retention or live listing evidence is supplied.
No consumer purchase or repeat-use evidence is supplied.
No measured cost or refund inputs are supplied.
No neighborhood-level supply-and-demand density data is supplied.
